Attorney · Executive Director · Advocate

From the
inside of the
system —
to changing it.

James White III is an attorney, Executive Director of the James For Change Foundation, and one of the most credible voices in criminal justice reform, because he has lived it. He has prosecuted cases, led statewide legal initiatives, and built programs that reach communities the system leaves behind. His work isn't just a message. It's infrastructure.

His Story

A legal career built on lived experience.

James White III's path to the law was not linear. After facing incarceration, he made a deliberate choice to pursue education as the vehicle for change, earning his Bachelor of Arts from Benedict College and his Juris Doctor from The Ohio State University Moritz College of Law.

What makes his law school years remarkable isn't just the degree: it's what he did while pursuing it: mentoring students, speaking inside prisons and juvenile detention centers, and personally assisting individuals with restoration of rights, record restrictions, and pardon applications.

He went on to handle arraignments, motions, bench trials, and jury trials as an Assistant Solicitor General in Fulton County. Today he chairs the BASICS Committee at the State Bar of Georgia, leads the James For Change Foundation as Executive Director, and continues to speak, advocate, and practice law.

"Prevention is the highest form of public safety."
Full biography
The James For Change Foundation

Dedicated to the prevention of young people's involvement with the criminal justice system, reducing recidivism, and promoting long-term community stability through legal education, mentorship, and direct advocacy, connecting those the system has left behind to resources, rights, and real second chances.

Criminal Justice Reform
Policy, lived experience, and the path to a more equitable system
Education as Prevention
How schools and access to opportunity interrupt the pipeline
Second Chances & Reentry
The legal landscape and human story of returning citizens
Law as a Tool for Change
Using legal expertise to advance community justice

He doesn't speak about the system.
He speaks from inside it.

James has addressed universities, juvenile detention centers, corporate audiences, and policymakers. He started speaking inside prisons before he even had his law degree. He brings a perspective that very few speakers can offer: the prosecutor who was once prosecuted.

Keynotes Available 45 min – Half Day In-Person & Virtual

Programs & For Schools

The Foundation's work on the ground.

All programs
01
School Outreach & Prevention
Direct engagement with students at risk of entering the juvenile justice system, building awareness, mentorship, and pathways before intervention becomes necessary.
Learn more
02
Reentry Legal Assistance
Connecting returning citizens to legal resources, record restriction guidance, pardon applications, and the support systems needed to rebuild a stable life.
Learn more
03
BASICS – State Bar of Georgia
As Committee Chair, James leads statewide initiatives connecting individuals with legal education, mentorship, and opportunity.
Learn more
